rationale: The Iran-US peace deal inching closer is the most relevant new development for Poland, as it could lower energy prices; the Russia-Ukraine war continues without qualitative change.

top_events.title: Iran signals Hormuz reopening tied to peace deal
top_events.summary: A US-Iran deal nearing signature could reopen Hormuz and ease global energy prices, offering indirect relief to Polish households and businesses still exposed to elevated energy costs.

top_events.title: Russia-Ukraine war ongoing
top_events.summary: War on Poland's eastern border continues with no major escalation or breakthrough this cycle — background security concern remains but no new qualitative change.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic for espionage
top_events.summary: Rising US-China tensions over espionage cases add marginal noise to global geopolitical risk, with limited direct impact on Poland's daily life.
